applicant_information.present_address: 12847 Donner Pass Rd, Apt 3B, Truckee, CA 96161

education.high_school_course_of_study: College-prep curriculum with emphasis on biology and physical education; completed AP Biology, AP English Language, and participated in outdoor recreation leadership elective focused on youth sports and basic coaching principles.
education.college_course_of_study: B.S. in Community Health Sciences with a minor in Outdoor Adventure Leadership; coursework includes Recreation Program Design, Aquatics Management, Lifespan Human Development, First Aid & Emergency Response, and Community Health Promotion.
education.college_type_of_degree_or_certificate_re: Bachelor of Science, Community Health Sciences (Minor: Outdoor Adventure Leadership)

skills_and_languages.other_training_or_work_related_skills:
Certifications:
- American Red Cross Lifeguarding/First Aid/CPR/AED (current; expires 2026-07-15). Includes training in water rescue techniques, spinal injury management, and use of rescue tubes and backboards.
- American Red Cross Water Safety Instructor (WSI), obtained 2022-04-10. Trained to teach preschool, youth, and adult swim lessons using Red Cross curriculum, skill progressions, and safety modules.
- Basic Life Support (BLS) for Healthcare Providers, American Heart Association, renewed 2024-09-30.

Aquatics & Recreation Skills:
- Three summers of experience as a lifeguard and swim instructor at a community pool, including guarding during open swim, lap swim, and special events (family nights, swim meets).
- Comfortable managing groups of 4–8 children in the water, ages 3–12, teaching basic water safety, floating, gliding, front crawl, backstroke, and breath control.
- Familiar with pool chemical safety basics (not a certified operator, but trained in recognizing irregular readings and reporting to supervisor) and daily opening/closing procedures.
- Experience enforcing facility rules consistently and respectfully, including redirecting unsafe behavior on pool decks, diving boards, and slides.

Youth Program & Customer Service Experience:
- Worked as a recreation aide for after-school programs, facilitating games, arts and crafts, and outdoor activities for kids 6–13; responsible for sign-in/out, snack distribution, and behavior management.
- Comfortable communicating with parents about their child’s progress in swim lessons and behavior during programs, including difficult conversations handled calmly and professionally.
- Experience working with children with mild sensory and developmental differences; adapt activities by breaking skills into smaller steps and providing extra reassurance.

Leadership & Teamwork:
- Frequently served as informal shift lead on busy days, helping set up guard rotations, ensuring rescue equipment was in place, and checking that safety checks were completed on time.
- Strong record of punctuality and reliability; in three summers at previous pool, never missed a scheduled shift without advance notice and coverage arranged.
- Able to stay calm and task-focused during emergencies; participated in several real minor first-aid incidents (scrapes, nosebleeds, minor head bumps) and one non-critical water rescue.

Technical & Administrative Skills:
- Comfortable using basic recreation registration software (previously used RecTrac) to check participants in, verify waivers, and look up schedules.
- Proficient with email, Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Forms), and basic incident reporting in standardized forms.
- Can create simple lesson plans and weekly progress notes for swim classes, including specific measurable goals (e.g., 10-yard unassisted front crawl, independent back float).

Personal Strengths:
- Bilingual (English/Spanish), which has been very helpful explaining safety rules and lesson progress to Spanish-speaking families.
- Physically fit and accustomed to standing, walking, and being outdoors for long periods in varying weather.
- Detail-oriented about safety checks, including scanning patterns, checking drains and main bodies of water, and monitoring high-risk areas like ladders and diving boards.
skills_and_languages.what_languages_do_you_speak_fluently_oth: Spanish (native level; grew up in a Spanish-speaking household and regularly communicate with family in Spanish). Can comfortably explain safety rules, emergency procedures, and swim instructions in Spanish to children and parents.

employment_history.employment_1_name_address_phone: Truckee Community Aquatic Center, 8924 Donner Pass Rd, Truckee, CA 96161, Phone: (530) 555-7321
employment_history.employment_1_your_position_duties_superv:
Position: Lifeguard & Swim Instructor
Duties: Provide vigilant patron surveillance during open swim, lap swim, and special events using established scanning patterns; enforce pool rules and respond to emergencies following facility Emergency Action Plan. Teach group and private swim lessons for children ages 3–12 and beginner adults using Red Cross-aligned curriculum, maintain accurate attendance and skills progress records, and communicate progress to parents. Assist with daily pool opening and closing, including deck and locker room checks, setting up lane lines, and putting away equipment. Complete incident/accident reports as needed and participate in monthly in-service training for rescue skills, CPR, and first aid.
Supervisor: Jordan Lee, Aquatics Coordinator
employment_history.employment_1_explain_your_reason_for_lea: Currently employed. Applying for additional seasonal hours with the Truckee-Donner Recreation & Park District to expand my involvement with community aquatics and youth recreation programs during the upcoming busy season.
employment_history.employment_2_name_address_phone: Meadowbrook Community Pool, 7201 35th Ave NE, Seattle, WA 98115, Phone: (206) 555-3842
employment_history.employment_2_your_position_duties_superv:
Position: Seasonal Lifeguard & Swim Instructor
Duties: Monitored indoor and outdoor pool areas, enforcing safety rules and maintaining a safe environment during open swim, lap swim, and special events. Performed rescues and first aid as needed, though most incidents were minor (slips, minor cuts, and assisting tired swimmers to safety). Taught group swim lessons for preschool (ages 3–5) and youth levels 1–3, focusing on water comfort, floating, basic strokes, and safe entry/exit techniques. Prepared simple lesson plans, tracked participant progress, and communicated with parents at the end of each session about strengths and areas for improvement. Assisted with locker room checks, deck cleaning, and lost-and-found organization.
Supervisor: Denise Howard, Aquatics Manager
employment_history.employment_2_explain_your_reason_for_lea: Seasonal position tied to summer schedule; left at the end of each summer to return to university in Nevada. Final departure in August 2022 was due to relocation to the Reno/Truckee area for my last year of college and subsequent work opportunities closer to school.
employment_history.employment_3_name_address_phone: Reno Youth Recreation Center, 2145 Riverside Dr, Reno, NV 89503, Phone: (775) 555-6610
employment_history.employment_3_your_position_duties_superv:
Position: Recreation Aide (After-School & Youth Programs)
Duties: Assisted in planning and leading after-school activities for children ages 6–13, including outdoor games, basic sports (soccer, basketball, kickball), arts and crafts, and homework support. Responsible for daily sign-in and sign-out procedures, ensuring that only authorized adults picked up participants. Helped manage group behavior using positive reinforcement and clear expectations, and documented any significant incidents or behavior concerns. Set up and broke down program spaces, sanitized shared equipment, and assisted with small snack preparation and distribution. Occasionally helped with special events such as family nights, holiday celebrations, and youth recreation fairs.
Supervisor: Marcus Nguyen, Youth Programs Supervisor
employment_history.employment_3_explain_your_reason_for_lea: Left position upon graduation from the University of Nevada, Reno, and relocation to Truckee in May 2023 to be closer to the mountains and pursue more aquatics- and outdoor-focused recreation work.
employment_history.employment_4_name_address_phone: Campus Recreation Center, University of Nevada, Reno, 1664 N Virginia St, Reno, NV 89557, Phone: (775) 555-2048
employment_history.employment_4_your_position_duties_superv:
Position: Front Desk Attendant & Facility Assistant
Duties: Checked students and community members into the recreation center using ID verification and membership software, answered questions about facility hours, intramural sports, and fitness classes, and provided basic tours to new members. Processed day passes, equipment check-outs (basketballs, racquetball gear, etc.), and locker rentals. Assisted with light cleaning of lobby and fitness areas, regularly wiped down surfaces, and restocked cleaning supplies. Helped set up and break down small events and intramural sign-up tables and occasionally assisted with basic administrative tasks like posting updated schedules and flyers.
Supervisor: Kelly Thompson, Facility Operations Coordinator
employment_history.employment_4_explain_your_reason_for_lea: Left the position to focus on off-campus youth recreation work that better aligned with my major and to free up time for upper-division coursework and field placements.
